title: Honorverse Hyperspace Model
description: First-cut schema (v0.1.0) for the hyperspace travel model: the discrete hyper bands (with apparent-velocity multipliers), translation mechanics, and the hyper-limit table by spectral class. Gravity waves, the Tellerman/grav-wave 'sail' riding, and dimensional shear are deliberately deferred. Canon values are flagged canon:true; interpolated/invented values (e.g. the Eta band speed) are canon:false with a documented method.

canonFlag.description: true = established in canon; false = interpolated/invented by us.

canon.description: Whether the band's existence is canon (all listed bands are).

entry_velocity_limit_c.description: Max real velocity (in c) at which a ship may translate INTO this band (Alpha = 0.3c; exceeding is fatal).

apparent_velocity_c.description: Apparent velocity (multiple of c relative to n-space). 'nominal_c' is the model value the flight planner uses; 'canon_datapoints' are verbatim canon examples (note their real-velocity context).

hyperLimit.description: The radius (light-minutes) around a star inside which hyper translation is fatal. A function of mass / spectral class. Ships must be BEYOND this to translate in or out.

by_spectral_class.description: Spectral class -> hyper limit in light-minutes (canon table).

applied_examples.description: Built-system stars mapped to their looked-up hyper limit (convenience; the lookup itself is canon-exact).
